channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Amazon doesn’t want the blame for the Post Office going under
item.description: Amazon is going on the defensive after a report from The Wall Street Journal revealed that the ecommerce giant plans to slash shipments with the United States Postal Service, which said this week...

item.title: Congress considers blowing up internet law
item.description: Internet platforms' liability shield Section 230 faced another round of attack at a Senate Commerce Committee hearing on Wednesday, this time with two distinct undercurrents complicating the...

item.title: How Did AI Learn to Be Nice? The Humans Behind the Curtain
item.description: Welcome back to AI From Scratch. This is Day 8/30 of the Understanding Beginner AI Series Where we are: Days 1–5: how the brain works — tokens, weights, transformers, attention. Day 6: why bigger...

item.title: How to Execute Cost Optimization Recommendations
item.description: We often think of the process of optimizing resources and technology infrastructure as a constant battle between FinOps teams and technology teams (and sometimes it is). However, after several...

item.title: Datacenter Proxies: Dead or Evolved? Use Cases in 2026
item.description: It's 2026, and if you've been in the scraping or data intelligence game for more than a few years, you remember the "Golden Age" of server IPs. You remember when a simple AWS or DigitalOcean...

item.title: J2ME — Building Java Mobile Games Under 128 kB
item.description: 🎬The Past… Does anyone remember downloading J2ME games via premium SMS, often discovered in the back pages of paper magazines? That’s where my professional game development journey began. Here are...

item.title: How I solved Claude Code's biggest missing feature
item.description: If you use Claude Code with more than one account, you know the pain. Switch from work to personal? /logout, /login, lose your session state, watch your MCP servers vanish. Do it again tomorrow....

item.title: What Makes a Good Open Source PR (Lessons From Getting Mine Closed)
item.description: My first real open source PR got closed. Not merged — closed. Someone else's fix got picked instead. And honestly? They deserved it. Here's the story, and what I took away from it. The Bug I was...

item.title: Detecting LLM Agent Contradictions Using NLI and Total Variance — A Python Implementation
item.description: LLM agents are non-deterministic. Everyone knows this. What is less discussed is a specific failure mode that is worse than variance — when an agent does not just give different answers across...

item.title: Protect Your Server: An In-Depth Look at the OpenClaw DCG Guard Plugin
item.description: Securing Your Development Environment with DCG Guard As AI-powered development agents become more integrated into our workflows, the risk of accidental, irreversible commands grows significantly....

item.title: Nothing CEO Carl Pei says smartphone apps will disappear as AI agents take their place
item.description: Nothing CEO Carl Pei says AI agents will eventually replace apps, shifting smartphones toward systems that understand intent and act on a user's behalf.

item.title: Underrated Postgres: Extended Statistics for Better Execution Plans
item.description: One of the features of Postgres which is very-little known and even less used (at least I rarely see it in production) is extended statistics to cover and encode the dependencies, relations,...

item.title: 🔱 Gemini 3.1 Pro vs. Claude 4.6: The Battle for Agentic Sovereignty
item.description: The benchmark war is over, and it ended in a stalemate. A deep dive into the March 2026 AI landscape. Compare Gemini’s Thinking Tiers and Antigravity vs. Claude’s Adaptive Effort and Cowork VM....

item.title: Hoto’s powerful PixelDrive electric screwdriver is 25 percent off
item.description: Hoto makes electric screwdrivers for different budgets. If you don’t want to spend much, you can get its most basic model, which we’ve recommended many times. That one comes with 25 bits, and has...

item.title: Nvidia is quietly building a multibillion-dollar behemoth to rival its chips business
item.description: Nvidia's networking business raked in $11 billion last quarter despite getting significantly less fanfare than chips and gaming.

item.title: The best deals on MacBooks right now
item.description: It’s 2026, and that means Apple sells MacBooks equipped with not just its capable M-series processors but A-series chips that were previously exclusive to the iPhone. With the MacBook Neo starting...

item.title: David Sacks’ big Iran warning gets big time ignored
item.description: Hello and welcome to Regulator, a newsletter for Verge subscribers about the politics of technology and the technology of politics - now landing in your inbox on Wednesdays! If someone has...

item.title: Robinhood is making a social network
item.description: Robinhood is now beta testing a Twitter-like social media platform it calls Robinhood Social. The trading-focused platform was announced last September at the company's HOOD Summit, but it's...

item.title: ChatGPT did not cure a dog’s cancer
item.description: When an Australian tech entrepreneur with no background in biology or medicine said ChatGPT helped save his dog from cancer, the story spread with the kind of validation Big Tech has long craved:...

item.title: Why Walmart and OpenAI Are Shaking Up Their Agentic Shopping Deal
item.description: After OpenAI’s Instant Checkout feature fell short, Walmart is instead embedding its Sparky chatbot directly into ChatGPT and Google Gemini.

item.title: Patreon CEO calls AI companies’ fair use argument ‘bogus,’ says creators should be paid
item.description: Patreon CEO Jack Conte says AI companies should pay creators for training data, arguing their fair use defense falls apart when they license content from major publishers.

item.title: Kasa’s Matter-compatible smart plugs are on sale for $11 a pop
item.description: If you’re looking for an easy and affordable way to add smarts to your home, you can buy two Kasa Smart Wi-Fi Plug Slim smart plugs on sale for $21.99 ($8 off) at Amazon and B&H Photo. That’s the...

item.title: Federal cyber experts called Microsoft's cloud a "pile of shit," approved it anyway
item.description: One Microsoft product was approved despite years of concerns about its security.

item.title: My favorite robot vacuum now supports Matter
item.description: Matic, my current top pick for the best robot vacuum, just got a big update. The unique-looking floor bot now works with Google Home and Apple Home, with support coming through the smart home...

item.title: OFAC Sanctions DPRK IT Worker Network Funding WMD Programs Through Fake Remote Jobs
item.description: The U.S. Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) has sanctioned six individuals and two entities for their involvement in the Democratic People's Republic of Korea...

item.title: Rebel Audio is a new AI podcasting tool aimed at first-time creators
item.description: Rebel Audio is a new all-in-one podcasting tool that allows creators to record podcasts, edit, clip content for social, and publish episodes, all without ever leaving the platform.

item.title: The Gemini-powered features in Google Workspace that are worth using
item.description: From summarizing emails, drafting content, organizing data, and tracking meetings, here are all the best Gemini features in Google Workspace.

item.title: The leaderboard “you can’t game,” funded by the companies it ranks
item.description: Artificial intelligence models are multiplying fast, and competition is stiff. With so many players crowding the space, which one will be the best — and who decides that? Arena, formerly LM Arena,...

item.title: This startup wants to make enterprise software look more like a prompt
item.description: The company has raised $12 million in seed funding to build an AI operating system for enterprise.

item.title: Interlock Ransomware Exploits Cisco FMC Zero-Day CVE-2026-20131 for Root Access
item.description: Amazon Threat Intelligence is warning of an active Interlock ransomware campaign that's exploiting a recently disclosed critical security flaw in Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center (FMC)...
